ac-2.title: Enable MFA for AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) users that have a console password

ac-2.description: Requires IAM user password policies to implement strong configurations, preventing the use of weak passwords and reducing the risk of unauthorized access.

si-7.description: Use AWS Signer to digitally sign code and verify software integrity

si-7.title: Enable EC2 Instance Metadata Service v2 (IMDSv2)
si-7.description: Use IMDSv2 to protect instance metadata from unauthorized access and ensure integrity

ac-5.title: Separation of Duties
ac-5.description: Implement separation of duties using IAM roles and policies
ac-5.implementation_guidance: Use IAM roles to separate administrative functions. Create distinct roles for different job functions (e.g., SecurityAdmin, DatabaseAdmin, NetworkAdmin). Use AWS Organizations SCPs to enforce separation at the organizational level. Monitor role usage with CloudTrail.

ac-7.title: Unsuccessful Logon Attempts
ac-7.description: Enforce account lockout after failed login attempts
ac-7.implementation_guidance: For application users: Configure Cognito user pools with account lockout policies. For AWS Console: Use CloudWatch Events to detect failed console logins and trigger Lambda functions to disable IAM users after threshold. Implement custom authentication with lockout logic in your applications.

ac-7.title: Implement MFA for the AWS root account
ac-7.description: Enable MFA for the root account to reduce brute-force and unauthorized access risk.

ac-7.title: Enable hardware MFA for the root account
ac-7.description: Enable hardware MFA for the root account to protect against unauthorized access.

ac-7.title: Enforce account password policy for IAM users
ac-7.description: Define strong password parameters within the AWS account password policy.

ac-7.title: Enable MFA for IAM console users
ac-7.description: Enable MFA for IAM users with console passwords to strengthen authentication.

ac-8.title: System Use Notification
ac-8.implementation_guidance: Display banner messages in your applications before authentication. Use Cognito pre-authentication Lambda triggers to present terms of use. Add consent banners to web applications served through CloudFront. Include usage notifications in API Gateway responses.

ac-8.title: Restrict and monitor the use of AWS root account
ac-8.description: Restrict and monitor root user usage to support system use notification requirements.

ac-11.title: Session Lock
ac-11.description: Implement automatic session lock after inactivity
ac-11.implementation_guidance: Configure Cognito token expiration and refresh token policies. Implement session timeout in your application code. Use ALB session stickiness with appropriate timeout values. For web apps, implement client-side session timeout with automatic logout.

ac-11.title: Enable Amazon S3 bucket object lock
ac-11.description: Enable object lock in S3 buckets to protect against unintentional changes or deletions.

ac-11.title: Block public access to Amazon S3 buckets
ac-11.description: Configure S3 to block public access to protect data from unauthorized access.

ac-12.title: Session Termination
ac-12.description: Automatically terminate sessions after defined conditions
ac-12.implementation_guidance: Set Cognito access token lifetime (default 1 hour, max 24 hours). Implement server-side session management with DynamoDB TTL for automatic cleanup. Use Lambda functions to enforce session termination policies based on time or conditions.

ac-12.title: Configure custom TLS policies for ELB listeners
ac-12.description: Configure custom TLS policies for ELB listeners to manage session security.

ac-12.title: Enable encryption for data in transit for DMS endpoints
ac-12.description: Enable encryption for DMS endpoints to prevent unauthorized access during data transfer.

ac-14.title: Permitted Actions Without Identification
ac-14.description: Identify and control actions permitted without authentication
ac-14.implementation_guidance: Document all unauthenticated endpoints in API Gateway. Use S3 bucket policies to explicitly define public access. Implement least privilege for unauthenticated Lambda functions. Review CloudFront distributions for public content. Use AWS Config to detect unintended public access.

ac-18.title: Wireless Access
ac-18.description: Establish wireless access restrictions and controls
ac-18.implementation_guidance: For AWS Workspaces: Configure wireless network policies. For remote access: Use AWS Client VPN with certificate-based authentication. Avoid wireless for production AWS access - use Direct Connect or VPN over wired connections. Document wireless usage policies for accessing AWS Console.

ac-19.title: Access Control for Mobile Devices
ac-19.implementation_guidance: Use AWS WorkSpaces for secure mobile access to corporate resources. Implement IAM policies that restrict access based on device compliance. Use AppStream 2.0 for application streaming to mobile devices. Require MFA for mobile device access to AWS Console.

ac-20.title: Use of External Systems
ac-20.description: Control use of external information systems
ac-20.implementation_guidance: Use IAM policies to restrict access from external systems. Implement SCPs in AWS Organizations to control cross-account access. Monitor external access with CloudTrail. Use VPC endpoints to keep traffic within AWS network. Document approved external systems and access methods.

ac-21.title: Information Sharing
ac-21.description: Control information sharing with external parties
ac-21.implementation_guidance: Use S3 bucket policies with time-limited presigned URLs for external sharing. Implement AWS Transfer Family for secure file transfers. Use IAM roles for cross-account access with external AWS accounts. Enable S3 Object Lock for immutable sharing. Monitor sharing with CloudTrail and S3 Access Logs.

ac-22.title: Publicly Accessible Content
ac-22.implementation_guidance: Use S3 Block Public Access at account and bucket level. Implement approval workflow for making content public. Use CloudFront with OAI for controlled public access. Enable AWS Config rules to detect public S3 buckets. Review public content regularly with Access Analyzer.

pm-1.title: Information Security Program Plan
pm-1.description: Develop and maintain information security program plan
pm-1.implementation_guidance: Document enterprise-wide security program. Use AWS Organizations for multi-account governance. Enable Security Hub across all accounts. Use Audit Manager for compliance tracking. Define security roles and responsibilities. Establish security metrics and reporting. Review program plan annually.

pm-2.title: Senior Information Security Officer
pm-2.implementation_guidance: Designate CISO or equivalent role. Grant appropriate IAM permissions for security oversight. Use Organizations for centralized security management. Document security officer responsibilities. Establish reporting structure. Provide access to Security Hub, GuardDuty, and audit tools.

pm-3.title: Information Security Resources
pm-3.implementation_guidance: Allocate budget for AWS security services. Use Cost Explorer to track security spending. Set up Budgets for security service costs. Include: Security Hub, GuardDuty, Config, CloudTrail, WAF, Shield. Plan for security tools, training, and staff. Review resource allocation quarterly.

pm-4.title: Plan of Action Process
pm-4.implementation_guidance: Establish process for tracking security weaknesses. Use Security Hub findings as input. Create OpsItems in Systems Manager for remediation tracking. Automate ticket creation with Lambda. Define SLAs for remediation by severity. Generate monthly POA&M reports.

pm-5.title: Information System Inventory
pm-5.implementation_guidance: Use Config to inventory all AWS resources. Implement tagging strategy (Environment, Owner, Application, DataClassification). Use Resource Groups to organize systems. Systems Manager Inventory for EC2 details. Create inventory reports with Athena. Update inventory automatically with Config.

pm-6.title: Information Security Measures of Performance
pm-6.implementation_guidance: Define security KPIs: time to detect/respond, vulnerability remediation rate, compliance score, security findings trend. Create CloudWatch dashboards. Use Security Hub security score. Build QuickSight dashboards for executive reporting. Review metrics monthly.

pm-7.title: Enterprise Architecture
pm-7.implementation_guidance: Design multi-account architecture with Organizations. Implement landing zone with Control Tower. Use Service Catalog for approved architectures. Define security zones and network segmentation. Document reference architectures. Include security in architecture review board.

pm-8.title: Critical Infrastructure Plan
pm-8.implementation_guidance: Identify critical systems and data. Enable Shield Advanced for DDoS protection. Implement WAF for application protection. Use Route 53 for DNS resilience. Deploy across multiple regions. Implement backup and recovery. Document critical infrastructure dependencies.

pm-9.title: Risk Management Strategy
pm-9.implementation_guidance: Document risk management framework. Use Security Hub for risk visibility. Enable GuardDuty for threat detection. Run Inspector for vulnerability assessment. Define risk appetite and tolerance. Implement risk register in DynamoDB. Review risks quarterly.

pm-10.title: Security Authorization Process
pm-10.implementation_guidance: Define authorization process and criteria. Use Audit Manager for evidence collection. Store authorization packages in S3. Define authorization boundaries. Establish continuous monitoring requirements. Set reauthorization schedule. Document authorization decisions.

pm-11.title: Mission/Business Process Definition
pm-11.implementation_guidance: Document business processes supported by AWS. Map processes to AWS services and accounts. Define criticality and recovery objectives. Use Service Catalog to provision approved solutions. Document process dependencies. Include in business continuity planning.

pm-12.title: Insider Threat Program
pm-12.implementation_guidance: Enable CloudTrail in all accounts. Use GuardDuty for anomalous behavior detection. Enable Macie for data exfiltration detection. Use Detective for investigation. Monitor privileged user activity. Implement user behavior analytics. Define insider threat indicators.

pm-13.title: Information Security Workforce
pm-13.implementation_guidance: Define security roles and responsibilities. Create IAM roles for security functions. Use Organizations for centralized security team access. Provide security team training and certifications. Document security team structure. Establish on-call rotation for security incidents.

pm-14.title: Testing, Training, and Monitoring
pm-14.implementation_guidance: Conduct regular security assessments. Use Inspector for vulnerability scanning. Enable GuardDuty for continuous monitoring. Run penetration tests (with AWS approval). Implement security testing in CI/CD. Monitor with Security Hub. Document testing results.

pm-15.title: Contacts with Security Groups
pm-15.implementation_guidance: Maintain AWS Enterprise Support relationship. Subscribe to AWS Security Bulletins. Join AWS security community forums. Establish relationships with AWS Security team. Participate in AWS security events. Document external security contacts. Share threat intelligence.

pm-16.title: Threat Awareness Program
pm-16.implementation_guidance: Subscribe to AWS security advisories. Enable GuardDuty for threat intelligence. Use Security Hub for threat aggregation. Create SNS topics for threat notifications. Share threat information with security team. Conduct threat briefings. Update defenses based on threats.

ps-1.title: Personnel Security Policy
ps-1.implementation_guidance: Document personnel security requirements. Define background check requirements. Establish access provisioning/deprovisioning procedures. Document acceptable use policies. Define security training requirements. Store policies in S3 with versioning.

ps-2.title: Position Risk Designation
ps-2.implementation_guidance: Classify positions by risk level (High, Moderate, Low). Map IAM roles to position risk levels. Apply tags to IAM users indicating risk designation. Define additional requirements for high-risk positions (background checks, training). Document position risk designations.

ps-3.title: Personnel Screening
ps-3.implementation_guidance: Conduct background checks before granting AWS access. Document screening requirements by position risk. Store screening records securely in S3. Verify identity before creating IAM users. Implement rescreening schedule. Document screening procedures.

ps-4.title: Personnel Termination
ps-4.implementation_guidance: Disable IAM users immediately upon termination. Revoke MFA devices and access keys. Remove from IAM groups. Rotate credentials they had access to. Use Lambda to automate termination workflow. Conduct exit interviews. Document termination procedures.

ps-5.title: Personnel Transfer
ps-5.implementation_guidance: Review and adjust IAM permissions upon role change. Remove access no longer needed. Add new required permissions. Update IAM groups and roles. Document transfer procedures. Monitor access changes with CloudTrail. Conduct access recertification.

ps-6.title: Access Agreements
ps-6.implementation_guidance: Require signed NDAs and acceptable use agreements. Store signed agreements in S3. Track agreement status in DynamoDB. Use Lambda to enforce agreement before IAM user creation. Include AWS-specific terms. Review and update agreements annually.

ps-7.title: Third-Party Personnel Security
ps-7.implementation_guidance: Define security requirements for contractors and vendors. Use separate IAM accounts or roles for third parties. Implement time-limited access. Require MFA for third-party access. Monitor third-party activity with CloudTrail. Document third-party agreements.

ps-8.title: Personnel Sanctions
ps-8.implementation_guidance: Define sanctions for security policy violations. Monitor for violations with CloudTrail and GuardDuty. Document violation investigation procedures. Implement progressive discipline. Disable access during investigations. Document sanctions process.

pt-1.title: PII Processing Policy
pt-1.implementation_guidance: Document PII handling requirements. Use Macie to discover and classify PII. Encrypt PII with KMS. Implement data minimization. Define PII retention periods. Use S3 lifecycle policies for PII deletion. Document PII processing activities.

pt-2.title: Authority to Process PII
pt-2.implementation_guidance: Document legal basis for PII processing. Obtain consent where required. Use IAM policies to restrict PII access. Implement purpose limitation. Use Macie to monitor PII usage. Document data processing agreements. Review authority annually.

ra-2.title: Security Categorization
ra-2.implementation_guidance: Implement FIPS 199 categorization (Low, Moderate, High). Use tags to mark system categorization. Apply tags to accounts, resources, and data. Use Macie to classify data sensitivity. Document categorization decisions. Review categorizations annually. Adjust controls based on categorization.

ra-3.title: Risk Assessment
ra-3.implementation_guidance: Conduct annual risk assessments. Use Security Hub for security posture. Run Inspector for vulnerabilities. Review Trusted Advisor recommendations. Assess threats, vulnerabilities, likelihood, and impact. Document risk assessment results. Update risk register. Implement risk treatment plans.

sa-1.title: System and Services Acquisition Policy
sa-1.implementation_guidance: Document security requirements for new systems. Use Service Catalog for approved solutions. Define security review process for acquisitions. Include security in procurement decisions. Document vendor security requirements. Review acquisition policy annually.

sa-2.title: Allocation of Resources
sa-2.description: Determine security requirements and allocate resources
sa-2.implementation_guidance: Include security in project planning. Allocate budget for security controls. Use Budgets to track security spending. Plan for: encryption, monitoring, backup, DR, security tools. Document resource allocation decisions. Review security budget quarterly.

sa-3.title: System Development Life Cycle
sa-3.implementation_guidance: Integrate security into SDLC. Use CodePipeline for CI/CD with security gates. Run security scans in CodeBuild. Implement security testing in each phase. Use Infrastructure as Code with security reviews. Document SDLC security requirements.

sa-4.title: Acquisition Process
sa-4.implementation_guidance: Define security requirements for AWS Marketplace purchases. Review vendor security documentation. Require security certifications. Use Service Catalog for approved vendors. Document vendor security assessments. Include security in contracts.

sa-5.title: Information System Documentation
sa-5.implementation_guidance: Maintain architecture diagrams and documentation. Store documentation in S3 with encryption. Use CloudFormation templates as infrastructure documentation. Document in Systems Manager Parameter Store. Protect documentation with IAM policies. Update documentation with changes.

sa-8.title: Security Engineering Principles
sa-8.implementation_guidance: Follow AWS Well-Architected Framework Security Pillar. Implement defense in depth. Use least privilege. Enable logging and monitoring. Encrypt data at rest and in transit. Implement automated security. Use Well-Architected Tool for reviews.

sa-9.title: External Information System Services
sa-9.description: Require security controls for external services
sa-9.implementation_guidance: Document security requirements for third-party services. Review vendor security certifications. Use IAM roles for external service access. Implement least privilege for integrations. Monitor external service usage with CloudTrail. Document approved external services.

sa-10.title: Developer Configuration Management
sa-10.description: Require developers to perform configuration management
sa-10.implementation_guidance: Use CodeCommit for version control. Implement branch protection. Require code reviews. Use CloudFormation for infrastructure as code. Implement automated testing. Track configuration changes. Document development standards.

sa-11.title: Developer Security Testing
sa-11.implementation_guidance: Integrate security testing in CI/CD. Use SAST tools in CodeBuild. Run DAST against test environments. Use Inspector for container scanning. Implement dependency scanning. Require security testing before production. Document testing requirements.

sa-15.title: Development Process and Criteria
sa-15.implementation_guidance: Define secure coding standards. Use CodeGuru for code review. Implement security training for developers. Require code reviews. Use secure coding libraries. Document development security requirements. Conduct security code reviews.

sa-16.title: Developer-Provided Training
sa-16.implementation_guidance: Provide secure coding training. Cover AWS security best practices. Train on: IAM, encryption, secrets management, secure APIs. Use hands-on labs. Track training completion. Update training annually. Document training requirements.

sa-17.title: Developer Security Architecture
sa-17.implementation_guidance: Document security architecture for applications. Include: authentication, authorization, encryption, logging. Use CloudFormation to codify architecture. Store architecture docs in S3. Review architecture before deployment. Update with changes.

sc-5.title: Denial of Service Protection
sc-5.implementation_guidance: Enable Shield Standard (automatic). Consider Shield Advanced for critical apps. Use WAF rate limiting. Deploy CloudFront for DDoS mitigation. Use Route 53 for DNS resilience. Implement auto-scaling. Monitor with CloudWatch. Document DoS response procedures.

sc-15.title: Collaborative Computing Devices
sc-15.description: Prohibit remote activation of collaborative devices
sc-15.implementation_guidance: Document policies for video conferencing. Use Chime with security controls. Disable camera/mic when not in use. Use WorkSpaces for secure remote access. Implement meeting access controls. Document approved collaboration tools.

sc-15.title: Enable VPC flow logs
sc-15.description: Enable VPC flow logs to monitor collaborative computing device network traffic.

sc-15.title: Close VPC default security groups
sc-15.description: Close default security groups to restrict collaborative computing device access.

si-5.title: Security Alerts and Advisories
si-5.description: Receive and respond to security alerts
si-5.implementation_guidance: Subscribe to AWS Security Bulletins. Enable Personal Health Dashboard notifications. Use Security Hub for aggregated alerts. Create SNS topics for security notifications. Monitor AWS security advisories. Document alert response procedures. Review alerts daily.

si-6.title: Security Function Verification
si-6.implementation_guidance: Use Config rules to verify security controls. Implement Lambda functions for security testing. Create CloudWatch alarms for security failures. Test encryption, logging, access controls. Verify backups and DR. Document verification procedures. Test quarterly.

si-6.title: Enable ECR private image scanning
si-6.description: Enable ECR private image scanning to verify security function integrity.

si-6.title: Enable Inspector ECR scanning
si-6.description: Enable Inspector ECR scanning for automated security function verification.

si-6.title: Enable AWS Security Hub
si-6.description: Enable Security Hub to verify security function operation across AWS resources.

si-8.title: Spam Protection
si-8.implementation_guidance: Use SES with spam filtering for email. Implement WAF rules for form spam. Use API Gateway throttling. Implement CAPTCHA for public forms. Use rate limiting. Monitor for spam patterns. Document spam protection measures.

si-8.title: Ensure WAFv2 rule groups are not empty
si-8.description: Ensure WAFv2 rule groups contain rules to filter spam and malicious content.

si-8.title: Configure WAF web ACL rules
si-8.description: Configure WAF web ACLs to protect against spam and unwanted traffic.

si-10.title: Information Input Validation
si-10.implementation_guidance: Implement input validation in applications. Use WAF for SQL injection and XSS protection. Validate inputs in API Gateway. Use Lambda for custom validation. Implement allow-lists for inputs. Sanitize user inputs. Document validation requirements.

si-10.title: Ensure WAFv2 rule groups are not empty
si-10.description: Ensure WAFv2 rule groups contain rules for input validation.

si-10.title: Configure WAF web ACL rules
si-10.description: Configure WAF web ACLs to validate and filter malicious input.

si-11.title: Error Handling
si-11.description: Generate error messages without sensitive information
si-11.implementation_guidance: Implement secure error handling in applications. Avoid exposing stack traces to users. Log detailed errors to CloudWatch Logs. Use generic error messages for users. Implement error monitoring. Document error handling standards.

si-11.title: Ensure CloudWatch alarms cannot be disabled
si-11.description: Verify CloudWatch alarm actions are enabled to support error handling and alerting.

si-11.title: Enable CloudTrail integration with CloudWatch Logs
si-11.description: Integrate CloudTrail with CloudWatch for error detection and handling.

si-16.title: Memory Protection
si-16.implementation_guidance: Use managed services (Lambda, Fargate) with built-in protections. For EC2, enable ASLR and DEP. Use memory-safe languages. Implement bounds checking. Use AWS Graviton processors with memory tagging. Document memory protection measures.

si-16.title: Enable ECR private image scanning
si-16.description: Enable ECR image scanning to detect vulnerabilities that could affect memory protection.

si-16.title: Enable Inspector Lambda standard scanning
si-16.description: Enable Inspector Lambda scanning to detect memory protection vulnerabilities.

si-16.title: Enable GuardDuty malware protection
si-16.description: Enable GuardDuty malware protection to detect memory-based threats.

sr-1.title: Supply Chain Risk Management Policy
sr-1.implementation_guidance: Document supply chain security requirements. Review AWS Marketplace vendor security. Use AWS Artifact for compliance reports. Assess third-party service risks. Document approved vendors. Review supply chain risks quarterly.

sr-2.title: Supply Chain Risk Management Plan
sr-2.description: Develop supply chain risk management plan
sr-2.implementation_guidance: Create supply chain risk management plan. Identify critical suppliers and dependencies. Assess supplier security. Use Config to track third-party integrations. Document risk mitigation strategies. Review plan annually.

sr-3.title: Supply Chain Controls and Processes
sr-3.implementation_guidance: Implement controls for third-party access. Use IAM roles for vendor access. Monitor vendor activity with CloudTrail. Review Marketplace purchases. Conduct vendor security assessments. Document supply chain controls.

ca-9.title: Internal System Connections
ca-9.implementation_guidance: Document all internal connections between VPCs and services. Use VPC peering with least privilege security groups. Implement PrivateLink for service-to-service connections. Use Transit Gateway for hub-and-spoke architecture. Monitor connections with VPC Flow Logs. Review connections quarterly.

ca-9.title: Enable VPC flow logs
ca-9.description: Enable VPC flow logs to monitor internal system connections and network traffic.

ca-9.title: Enable VPC endpoint services
ca-9.description: Use VPC endpoints to manage and secure internal system connections.

ia-11.title: Re-authentication
ia-11.implementation_guidance: Require MFA for privileged operations. Implement step-up authentication in Cognito. Use IAM policies with MFA conditions. Require re-authentication for sensitive actions. Implement session timeout for privileged access. Use Lambda to enforce re-authentication policies.

ia-11.title: Enable MFA for IAM console users
ia-11.description: Enable MFA for IAM console users to support re-authentication requirements.

ia-11.title: Enable MFA for all IAM users
ia-11.description: Enable MFA for all IAM users to enforce re-authentication.

ia-12.title: Identity Proofing
ia-12.description: Require identity proofing for user registration
ia-12.implementation_guidance: Implement identity verification in Cognito user pools. Use Lambda for custom identity proofing logic. Consider Rekognition for document verification. Require multiple forms of identification. Document identity proofing procedures. Store verification records securely in S3.

ia-12.title: Enable MFA for IAM console users
ia-12.description: Enable MFA for IAM console users to support identity proofing.

ia-12.title: Enforce account password policy for IAM users
ia-12.description: Enforce strong password policies to support identity proofing requirements.

ir-2.title: Incident Response Training
ir-2.implementation_guidance: Conduct AWS-specific IR training. Use GameDay for hands-on exercises. Train on GuardDuty, Security Hub, CloudTrail analysis. Create test scenarios with CloudFormation. Practice EC2 forensics and S3 breach response. Document training completion. Conduct training annually.

ir-2.title: Enable GuardDuty malware protection
ir-2.description: Enable GuardDuty malware protection to support incident response training scenarios.

ir-2.title: Enable AWS Security Hub
ir-2.description: Enable Security Hub to provide incident data for response training.

mp-4.title: Media Storage
mp-4.description: Physically control and securely store media
mp-4.implementation_guidance: Store backups in S3 with encryption. Use Glacier for long-term retention. Enable S3 Object Lock for immutability. Use AWS Backup for centralized management. Implement cross-region replication. Document storage procedures. Review storage quarterly.

pl-8.title: Information Security Architecture
pl-8.implementation_guidance: Design multi-account security architecture. Use Control Tower for landing zone. Implement security zones and network segmentation. Follow Well-Architected Security Pillar. Document reference architectures. Use CloudFormation for infrastructure as code. Review architecture quarterly.

sc-10.title: Network Disconnect
sc-10.implementation_guidance: Configure ALB idle timeout (default 60 seconds). Set NLB idle timeout for TCP connections. Implement API Gateway timeout (29 seconds max). Configure session timeout in applications. Use Lambda to enforce connection limits. Document timeout policies.

sc-10.title: Configure custom TLS policies for ELB listeners
sc-10.description: Configure TLS policies for ELB to manage network session disconnection.

sc-10.title: Enable encryption for data in transit for DMS endpoints
sc-10.description: Enable DMS endpoint encryption to manage secure network disconnection.

sc-23.title: Session Authenticity
sc-23.implementation_guidance: Use TLS 1.2+ for all connections. Implement certificate pinning where appropriate. Use Certificate Manager for SSL/TLS certificates. Enable HTTPS-only with CloudFront. Configure ALB with secure listeners. Implement HSTS headers. Monitor certificate expiration.

sc-23.title: Configure custom TLS policies for ELB listeners
sc-23.description: Configure TLS policies for ELB to ensure session authenticity.

sc-23.title: Ensure WAFv2 rule groups are not empty
sc-23.description: Ensure WAFv2 rule groups contain rules to validate session authenticity.

sr-5.title: Acquisition Strategies
sr-5.description: Employ acquisition strategies to reduce supply chain risks
sr-5.implementation_guidance: Use AWS Marketplace for vetted vendors. Review vendor security certifications. Implement multi-vendor strategy. Use AWS services to reduce third-party dependencies. Document acquisition security requirements. Review vendor security annually.

sr-11.title: Component Authenticity
sr-11.implementation_guidance: Use ECR for trusted container images. Scan images with Inspector. Use CodeArtifact for package management. Verify software signatures. Use official AWS AMIs. Document trusted sources. Implement software bill of materials.

sr-12.title: Component Disposal
sr-12.implementation_guidance: Delete S3 objects and verify deletion. Delete EBS volumes and snapshots. Delete KMS keys to make encrypted data unrecoverable. Use S3 Object Lock delete markers. AWS handles physical media destruction. Document disposal procedures. Maintain deletion audit logs.

sa-22.title: Unsupported System Components
sa-22.implementation_guidance: Use Systems Manager to inventory software versions. Run Inspector to detect unsupported software. Create Config rules for version compliance. Implement patching schedule. Plan for end-of-life migrations. Document supported versions. Review quarterly.

cm-4.title: Impact Analyses
cm-4.implementation_guidance: Analyze security impact before changes. Use Config to track configuration changes. Test changes in non-production first. Use CloudFormation change sets to preview impacts. Document impact analysis procedures. Review high-impact changes with security team.

cm-9.title: Configuration Management Plan
cm-9.implementation_guidance: Document configuration management procedures. Use Config for baseline tracking. Implement CloudFormation for infrastructure as code. Use Systems Manager for configuration enforcement. Define change control process. Review plan annually.

cm-9.title: Enable AWS Security Hub
cm-9.description: Enable Security Hub to support configuration management monitoring.

cm-9.title: Restrict IAM policies with blacklisted actions
cm-9.description: Restrict IAM policies to enforce configuration management controls.

cm-10.title: Software Usage Restrictions
cm-10.implementation_guidance: Document approved software list. Use Systems Manager to inventory installed software. Create Config rules for unauthorized software. Use License Manager to track licenses. Implement software allow-listing. Monitor with Inspector.

cm-10.title: Restrict IAM policies with blacklisted actions
cm-10.description: Restrict IAM policies to enforce software usage restrictions.

cm-11.title: User-installed Software
cm-11.implementation_guidance: Restrict user ability to install software. Use Systems Manager to control installations. Provide approved software via AppStream. Implement least privilege for installations. Monitor unauthorized installations with Config. Document approval process.

cm-11.title: Restrict IAM policies with blacklisted actions
cm-11.description: Restrict IAM policies to control user-installed software.

cm-11.title: Enable AWS Security Hub
cm-11.description: Enable Security Hub to detect unauthorized software installations.

cm-12.title: Information Location
cm-12.implementation_guidance: Use Macie to discover sensitive data locations. Tag resources with data classification. Document where sensitive data resides. Implement geographic restrictions if needed. Use S3 inventory for data location tracking. Review data locations quarterly.

cm-12.title: Enable AWS Security Hub
cm-12.description: Enable Security Hub to track and monitor information location across AWS resources.

cp-3.title: Contingency Training
cp-3.implementation_guidance: Train personnel on DR procedures. Conduct DR exercises using CloudFormation. Practice failover procedures. Train on backup restoration. Document training completion. Conduct training annually. Test DR plans during training.

cp-3.title: Include EBS volumes in backup plans
cp-3.description: Include EBS volumes in AWS Backup plans to support contingency and recovery training.

cp-3.title: Include EFS file systems in backup plans
cp-3.description: Include EFS file systems in AWS Backup plans for contingency preparedness.

cp-3.title: Enforce backup recovery point minimum retention
cp-3.description: Enforce minimum retention for backup recovery points to support contingency operations.

cp-3.title: Encrypt backup recovery points
cp-3.description: Encrypt backup recovery points to protect contingency data.

ia-6.title: Authentication Feedback
ia-6.implementation_guidance: Implement generic error messages for failed authentication. Don't reveal whether username or password was incorrect. Use Cognito with secure feedback. Avoid account enumeration. Implement rate limiting. Document authentication feedback standards.

ia-6.title: Enable MFA for IAM console users
ia-6.description: Enable MFA for IAM console users to provide secure authentication feedback.

ia-6.title: Enable MFA for all IAM users
ia-6.description: Enable MFA for all IAM users to strengthen authentication mechanisms.

mp-7.title: Media Use
mp-7.description: Restrict use of certain types of media
mp-7.implementation_guidance: Define approved media types. Restrict USB access on WorkSpaces. Use AppStream for controlled application access. Implement DLP policies. Monitor media usage with CloudTrail. Document media use policies.

pl-1.title: Planning Policy and Procedures
pl-1.implementation_guidance: Document security planning procedures. Define system authorization process. Establish security architecture requirements. Store policies in S3 with versioning. Review policies annually. Communicate policies to stakeholders.

pl-11.title: Baseline Tailoring
pl-11.implementation_guidance: Document baseline tailoring decisions. Justify control modifications. Use Security Hub with custom standards. Implement tailored controls with Config rules. Document compensating controls. Review tailoring annually.

ps-9.title: Position Descriptions
ps-9.description: Incorporate security roles in position descriptions
ps-9.implementation_guidance: Define security responsibilities in job descriptions. Document required security training by role. Map positions to IAM roles. Store position descriptions in S3. Include security clearance requirements. Review descriptions annually.

ra-7.title: Risk Response
ra-7.description: Respond to findings from security assessments
ra-7.implementation_guidance: Establish risk response procedures. Use Security Hub for findings management. Create OpsItems for remediation. Define risk acceptance criteria. Document risk decisions. Track remediation with Systems Manager. Review risks quarterly.

ra-7.title: Enable AWS Security Hub
ra-7.description: Enable Security Hub to support risk response through centralized findings.

ra-7.title: Enable ECR private image scanning
ra-7.description: Enable ECR image scanning to identify risks requiring response.

ra-7.title: Enable Inspector ECR scanning
ra-7.description: Enable Inspector ECR scanning for automated risk identification.

ra-9.title: Criticality Analysis
ra-9.implementation_guidance: Identify mission-critical systems and data. Tag critical resources. Document dependencies. Use Config to track critical resources. Implement enhanced monitoring for critical systems. Review criticality quarterly.

ra-9.title: Enable AWS Security Hub
ra-9.description: Enable Security Hub to support criticality analysis of AWS resources.

ra-9.title: Enable GuardDuty malware protection
ra-9.description: Enable GuardDuty malware protection to assess criticality of detected threats.

sc-2.title: Separation of System and User Functionality
sc-2.implementation_guidance: Separate management and data planes. Use dedicated VPCs for management. Implement separate IAM roles for admin vs user functions. Use Organizations for account separation. Document separation architecture.

sc-2.title: Ensure WAFv2 rule groups are not empty
sc-2.description: Ensure WAFv2 rule groups contain rules to separate user functionality from system management.

sc-2.title: Configure WAF web ACL rules
sc-2.description: Configure WAF web ACLs to enforce separation of user and system functionality.

sc-4.title: Information in Shared Resources
sc-4.implementation_guidance: Encrypt data at rest with KMS. Use dedicated EBS volumes per tenant. Implement S3 bucket policies for isolation. Clear memory after use. Use encryption for data in transit. Document data isolation procedures.

sc-4.title: Close VPC default security groups
sc-4.description: Close VPC default security groups to prevent information leakage in shared resources.

sc-17.title: Public Key Infrastructure Certificates
sc-17.implementation_guidance: Use Certificate Manager for public certificates. Use Private CA for internal PKI. Implement certificate lifecycle management. Monitor certificate expiration with CloudWatch. Automate certificate renewal. Document PKI procedures.

sc-17.title: Check IAM server certificate expiration
sc-17.description: Monitor IAM server certificate expiration to maintain PKI certificate validity.

sc-17.title: Disable ACM PCA root CA
sc-17.description: Disable ACM PCA root CA when not in use to protect PKI infrastructure.

sc-18.title: Mobile Code
sc-18.description: Define acceptable and unacceptable mobile code
sc-18.implementation_guidance: Define mobile code policies (JavaScript, Java applets). Use WAF to control mobile code. Implement Content Security Policy headers. Use Lambda@Edge for code inspection. Document approved mobile code. Review policies annually.

sc-18.title: Ensure WAFv2 rule groups are not empty
sc-18.description: Ensure WAFv2 rule groups contain rules to control mobile code execution.

sc-18.title: Configure WAF web ACL rules
sc-18.description: Configure WAF web ACLs to filter and control mobile code.

sc-22.title: Architecture for Name/Address Resolution
sc-22.implementation_guidance: Use Route 53 with multiple name servers. Implement Route 53 Resolver for VPC DNS. Use health checks for failover. Deploy across multiple regions. Enable DNSSEC. Document DNS architecture.

sc-22.title: Enable Network Firewall multi-AZ deployment
sc-22.description: Enable multi-AZ Network Firewall to protect name resolution services.

sc-22.title: Enable VPC flow logs
sc-22.description: Enable VPC flow logs to monitor DNS and name resolution traffic.

sc-39.title: Process Isolation
sc-39.implementation_guidance: Use Lambda for isolated function execution. Use Fargate for container isolation. Implement EC2 with separate security groups. Use separate VPCs for isolation. Document isolation architecture.

sc-39.title: Close VPC default security groups
sc-39.description: Close default security groups to support process isolation between workloads.

sc-39.title: Enable VPC endpoint services
sc-39.description: Use VPC endpoints to isolate processes and limit network exposure.

si-12.title: Information Management and Retention
si-12.implementation_guidance: Define retention policies by data type. Use S3 lifecycle policies for automated retention. Use Glacier for long-term archival. Implement S3 Object Lock for compliance. Use AWS Backup for retention management. Document retention schedules.

si-12.title: Configure CloudWatch log group retention period
si-12.description: Set retention periods on CloudWatch log groups for information management.

si-12.title: Protect S3 resources with backup plans
si-12.description: Protect S3 resources with backup plans for information retention.

sr-6.title: Supplier Assessments
sr-6.implementation_guidance: Review AWS Marketplace vendor security. Request vendor security documentation. Review AWS Artifact for AWS compliance. Conduct vendor security assessments. Document assessment results. Review vendors annually.

sr-8.title: Notification Agreements
sr-8.implementation_guidance: Establish notification procedures with vendors. Use SNS for vendor notifications. Implement EventBridge for automated notifications. Document notification requirements in contracts. Test notification procedures. Review agreements annually.

sr-10.title: Inspection of Systems
sr-10.description: Inspect systems and components for tampering
sr-10.implementation_guidance: Use Inspector for vulnerability scanning. Monitor for unauthorized changes with Config. Use Systems Manager for integrity checking. Review CloudTrail for suspicious activity. Implement file integrity monitoring. Conduct inspections quarterly.

at-1.title: Security Awareness and Training Policy
at-1.description: Establish security awareness and training policies
at-1.implementation_guidance: Document security training requirements in your security policy. Use IAM to track user roles and required training. Maintain training records in a secure S3 bucket. Use CloudWatch Events to trigger training reminders. Integrate with your LMS or use AWS Partner solutions for training delivery.

at-2.title: Security Awareness Training
at-2.description: Provide security awareness training to all users
at-2.implementation_guidance: Deliver training materials via S3-hosted content or WorkDocs. Use Chime for virtual training sessions. Track completion with Lambda functions and DynamoDB. Cover AWS-specific topics: IAM best practices, MFA usage, phishing awareness, data classification, incident reporting.

at-3.title: Role-Based Security Training
at-3.implementation_guidance: Map IAM roles to training requirements. Provide specialized training for: AWS administrators (IAM, security services), developers (secure coding, secrets management), security team (GuardDuty, Security Hub, incident response). Track role-based training completion in DynamoDB.

at-4.title: Security Training Records
at-4.implementation_guidance: Store training records in S3 with encryption. Use DynamoDB to track completion dates and expiration. Create CloudWatch dashboards for training compliance. Set up Lambda functions to send reminders for expiring training. Generate compliance reports with Athena queries.

au-5.title: Response to Audit Processing Failures
au-5.implementation_guidance: Create CloudWatch alarms for CloudTrail delivery failures, S3 bucket access errors, and log storage capacity. Use SNS to alert security team. Implement Lambda functions to take corrective action (e.g., increase storage, fix permissions). Monitor CloudWatch Logs delivery with EventBridge rules.

au-5.title: Ensure CloudWatch alarms cannot be disabled
au-5.description: Verify CloudWatch alarm actions are enabled to support audit failure alerting.

au-5.title: Verify CloudWatch alarm settings
au-5.description: Verify CloudWatch alarm settings are properly configured for audit monitoring.

au-5.title: Enable CloudTrail integration with CloudWatch Logs
au-5.description: Send CloudTrail logs to CloudWatch for real-time audit failure monitoring and alerting.

au-7.title: Audit Reduction and Report Generation
au-7.description: Provide audit analysis and reporting capabilities
au-7.implementation_guidance: Use Athena to query CloudTrail logs in S3. Create QuickSight dashboards for audit visualization. Use CloudWatch Logs Insights for real-time analysis. Deploy OpenSearch for advanced log analytics. Create saved queries for common audit reports (failed logins, privilege escalation, data access).

au-7.title: Configure CloudWatch log group retention period
au-7.description: Set retention periods on CloudWatch log groups to support audit record management.

au-7.title: Enable Amazon S3 bucket logging
au-7.description: Enable S3 bucket logging to support audit record collection and analysis.

au-8.title: Time Stamps
au-8.description: Use synchronized time stamps for audit records
au-8.implementation_guidance: AWS services automatically use UTC timestamps. For EC2 instances, configure NTP using Amazon Time Sync Service (169.254.169.123). Verify time synchronization with Systems Manager. All CloudTrail events include precise timestamps. Use CloudWatch Logs timestamp for correlation across services.

au-8.title: Enable CloudTrail log file validation
au-8.description: Enable log file validation to ensure integrity and timestamping of audit records.

au-8.title: Enable CloudTrail integration with CloudWatch Logs
au-8.description: Integrate CloudTrail with CloudWatch for time-synchronized audit record processing.

ca-1.title: Security Assessment and Authorization Policies
ca-1.implementation_guidance: Document assessment procedures in your security policy. Use AWS Audit Manager to automate evidence collection. Define authorization boundaries for AWS accounts and workloads. Implement continuous monitoring with Security Hub and Config. Document system authorization decisions in S3 with versioning.

ca-3.title: System Interconnections
ca-3.implementation_guidance: Document all VPC peering connections, Transit Gateway attachments, and PrivateLink endpoints. Use VPC Flow Logs to monitor interconnection traffic. Implement security groups and NACLs at interconnection points. Review and approve new interconnections through change management. Monitor with CloudTrail and Config.

ca-3.title: Configure custom TLS policies for ELB listeners
ca-3.description: Configure TLS policies for ELB to secure information exchange between systems.

ca-3.title: Enable VPC endpoint services
ca-3.description: Use VPC endpoints to secure information exchange within AWS without traversing the internet.

ca-3.title: Enable AWS Security Hub
ca-3.description: Enable Security Hub to monitor and assess information exchange security posture.

ca-5.title: Plan of Action and Milestones
ca-5.description: Develop and maintain POA&M for security weaknesses
ca-5.implementation_guidance: Use Security Hub findings as input for POA&M. Track remediation in Systems Manager OpsCenter or integrate with Jira. Document milestones and responsible parties. Use Lambda to auto-create tickets for critical findings. Generate POA&M reports with Athena queries on Security Hub data.

ca-6.title: Security Authorization
ca-6.description: Assign senior official to authorize system operation
ca-6.implementation_guidance: Document authorization process and approving officials. Use AWS Audit Manager to collect authorization evidence. Store authorization packages in S3 with encryption and versioning. Implement IAM policies to restrict system access until authorized. Set authorization expiration dates with CloudWatch Events reminders.

ca-7.title: Continuous Monitoring
ca-7.description: Develop and implement continuous monitoring strategy
ca-7.implementation_guidance: Enable Security Hub for centralized monitoring. Use Config for configuration compliance. Enable GuardDuty for threat detection. Create CloudWatch dashboards for security metrics. Use Systems Manager for patch compliance. Implement automated remediation with Lambda. Generate monthly monitoring reports.

cm-5.title: Access Restrictions for Change
cm-5.description: Define and enforce access restrictions for changes
cm-5.implementation_guidance: Use IAM policies to restrict who can modify infrastructure. Implement SCPs in Organizations to prevent unauthorized changes. Require all changes through CloudFormation or Terraform. Use CodePipeline with approval stages. Enable MFA delete on S3 buckets. Monitor changes with CloudTrail and Config.

cm-5.title: Audit bucket policy changes prohibited
cm-5.description: Prevent unauthorized changes to audit bucket policies to maintain configuration integrity.

cm-5.title: Audit bucket retention policy
cm-5.description: Enforce retention policies on audit buckets to protect configuration change records.

cm-5.title: Restrict IAM policies with blacklisted actions
cm-5.description: Restrict IAM policies to prevent unauthorized configuration changes.

cp-8.title: Telecommunications Services
cp-8.implementation_guidance: Implement redundant connectivity: primary Direct Connect with VPN backup. Use multiple Direct Connect locations for diversity. Configure Route 53 health checks for automatic failover. Document telecommunications providers and SLAs. Test failover procedures quarterly. Consider multiple ISPs for VPN connections.

cp-8.title: Protect S3 resources with backup plans
cp-8.description: Protect S3 resources with backup plans for telecommunications service continuity.

cp-8.title: Include EBS volumes in backup plans
cp-8.description: Include EBS volumes in backup plans for service continuity.

cp-8.title: Enforce backup recovery point minimum retention
cp-8.description: Enforce minimum retention for recovery points to support service continuity.

ia-7.title: Cryptographic Module Authentication
ia-7.description: Implement authentication mechanisms for cryptographic modules
ia-7.implementation_guidance: Use AWS KMS with IAM policies for key access control. For FIPS 140-2 Level 3, use CloudHSM with client authentication. Implement key policies requiring multiple approvers for sensitive operations. Use CloudTrail to audit all cryptographic operations. Rotate CloudHSM credentials regularly.

ia-7.title: Enable EBS encryption by default
ia-7.description: Enable EBS encryption by default using validated cryptographic modules.

ia-7.title: Restrict KMS key policy public access
ia-7.description: Restrict KMS key policies to prevent public access to cryptographic modules.

ia-7.title: Encrypt backup recovery points
ia-7.description: Encrypt backup recovery points using validated cryptographic modules.

ir-3.title: Incident Response Testing
ir-3.implementation_guidance: Conduct tabletop exercises quarterly. Perform technical tests in isolated AWS accounts. Use CloudFormation to create test scenarios. Simulate incidents: compromised IAM credentials, data exfiltration, ransomware, DDoS. Use Lambda to inject test events. Document test results and lessons learned in S3.

ir-3.title: Enable GuardDuty malware protection
ir-3.description: Enable GuardDuty malware protection to support incident response testing.

ir-3.title: Encrypt backup recovery points
ir-3.description: Encrypt backup recovery points to support incident response and recovery testing.

ir-3.title: Enable AWS Security Hub
ir-3.description: Enable Security Hub to support incident response testing and validation.

ir-7.title: Incident Response Assistance
ir-7.implementation_guidance: Maintain AWS Enterprise Support for 24/7 incident assistance. Document AWS Support contact procedures. Create Systems Manager runbooks for common incidents. Establish relationships with AWS Security team. Use AWS Professional Services for complex incidents. Document internal and external support contacts.

ir-7.title: Enable AWS Security Hub
ir-7.description: Enable Security Hub to provide centralized incident response assistance.

ir-7.title: Enable GuardDuty malware protection
ir-7.description: Enable GuardDuty malware protection for automated incident detection assistance.

ma-1.title: System Maintenance Policy and Procedures
ma-1.implementation_guidance: Document maintenance procedures for AWS resources. Use Systems Manager Maintenance Windows for scheduled maintenance. Define approval requirements for maintenance activities. Track maintenance with Config and CloudTrail. Implement change management for infrastructure updates. Document emergency maintenance procedures.

mp-1.title: Media Protection Policy and Procedures
mp-1.implementation_guidance: Document policies for protecting data at rest. Require encryption for all storage: S3, EBS, RDS, EFS. Define data classification and handling procedures. Use S3 Object Lock for immutable storage. Implement backup retention policies with AWS Backup. Document media sanitization procedures (delete with verification).

mp-2.title: Media Access
mp-2.description: Restrict access to digital and non-digital media
mp-2.implementation_guidance: Use IAM policies to control S3 bucket access. Enable S3 Block Public Access. Encrypt data with KMS and control key access. Use Macie to discover sensitive data. Implement least privilege for EBS volume access. Monitor access with CloudTrail and S3 Access Logs. Use VPC endpoints to keep traffic private.

mp-3.title: Media Marking
mp-3.implementation_guidance: Use S3 object tags to mark data classification (Public, Internal, Confidential, Restricted). Apply tags to EBS volumes and snapshots. Use Macie to auto-classify and tag sensitive data. Implement tag-based IAM policies for access control. Create CloudWatch dashboards showing data classification distribution. Enforce tagging with Config rules.

mp-5.title: Media Transport
mp-5.description: Protect and control media during transport
mp-5.implementation_guidance: Use AWS Snowball for large data transfers with built-in encryption. Use DataSync for secure online transfers. Implement Transfer Family with encryption for file transfers. Use S3 Transfer Acceleration with TLS. For physical media transport, use AWS Import/Export with encryption. Document chain of custody procedures.

mp-6.title: Media Sanitization
mp-6.description: Sanitize media before disposal or reuse
mp-6.implementation_guidance: Delete S3 objects and verify deletion. Delete EBS volumes and snapshots. For encrypted data, delete KMS keys to make data unrecoverable. Use S3 Object Lock delete markers for audit trail. AWS handles physical media destruction per NIST 800-88. Document sanitization procedures and maintain deletion logs in CloudTrail.

pl-2.title: System Security Plan
pl-2.description: Develop and maintain system security plan
pl-2.implementation_guidance: Document security plan for each AWS workload/account. Use AWS Audit Manager templates as starting point. Include: system architecture, security controls, data flows, interconnections, responsible parties. Store plans in S3 with versioning. Use Systems Manager Parameter Store for configuration details. Review and update plans annually or after significant changes.

pl-4.title: Rules of Behavior
pl-4.description: Establish rules of behavior for system users
pl-4.implementation_guidance: Document acceptable use policies for AWS resources. Present rules during user onboarding. Use Cognito pre-authentication triggers to require acceptance. Store signed acknowledgments in S3. Include rules for: password management, MFA usage, data handling, incident reporting, remote access. Review and update rules annually.

pl-10.title: Baseline Selection
pl-10.implementation_guidance: Select appropriate NIST 800-53 baseline (Low, Moderate, High). Enable Security Hub standards (AWS Foundational, CIS, PCI-DSS). Use Config conformance packs for baseline enforcement. Document baseline selection rationale. Implement baseline controls with CloudFormation. Use Audit Manager to track baseline compliance.

ca-2.title: Control Assessments
ca-2.description: Develop and implement control assessment procedures
ca-2.implementation_guidance: Conduct annual security control assessments. Use Security Hub for continuous assessment. Enable Config rules for automated control testing. Use Audit Manager to manage assessment process. Run Inspector for vulnerability assessments. Document assessment procedures and results. Create assessment reports with findings and recommendations. Track remediation of assessment findings.
